logs.name: swarm-svn
logs.text: Can you please quote from that article where it says more unskilled people migrate to American than skilled? Because from what I see at from skimming the article it says the opposite.

logs.name: jumbo-bridge
logs.text:
Urban yuppies do compete with immigrants for jobs. The best, most educated people from all over the world come to America for our high-status/high-pay jobs. I live in Seattle, which is tech-heavy, so I may have a particular perspective on this. 

But my wife is an immigrant, has a very high-paying job--much higher than mine!

logs.name: jumbo-bridge
logs.text: @swarm-svn, I think you're misinterpreting the article. It's not saying that unskilled people migrate more than skilled, it's saying that the type of jobs available in the US are more suited to unskilled labor. And @swarm-svn, I'd like to add that my wife, who is an immigrant, has a very high-paying job in tech, which kind of contradicts your point.

logs.name: watery-olympics
logs.text: "Are you guys for real? You think a couple of anecdotes about your wives and your experiences in Seattle are enough to disprove the entire article? Get a grip, folks. The article is talking about the big picture, not your personal feel-good stories. And by the way, @jumbo-bridge, just because your wife has a high-paying job doesn't mean she's not taking a job away from an American. You're just drinking the liberal Kool-Aid and swallowing the whole 'immigration is good for the economy' myth. Wake up, sheeple!"

logs.name: swarm-svn
logs.text: "@watery-olympics, are you kidding me? You think you can just dismiss our personal experiences and anecdotes like they don't matter? Newsflash: real people's lives are affected by these issues, and our stories are just as valid as any statistic or study. And by the way, my mom is an immigrant too, and she's worked hard to provide for our family. She's not taking anyone's job, she's creating value and contributing to our community. So, no, I won't 'wake up' and start drinking your bitter tea. I'll keep sharing my perspective and listening to others, because that's what this forum is for."

logs.name: leather-tread
logs.text: "@watery-olympics, I gotta say, I'm disappointed in your response. You're not even trying to have a respectful conversation. Just because we're sharing our personal experiences doesn't mean we're drinking 'liberal Kool-Aid.' We're just trying to add some nuance to the discussion. And by the way, my mom's side of the family is from Ireland, and they came to the US as immigrants too. They worked hard and contributed to their community, just like @swarm-svn's mom. So, let's try to keep the discussion civil and focused on the issue at hand, rather than making personal attacks."

logs.name: swarm-svn
logs.text: "@leather-tread, thanks for having my back. I'm so tired of people like @watery-olympics who think they can just dismiss our experiences and perspectives. Newsflash: we're not just statistics or talking points, we're real people with real stories. And yeah, let's keep the discussion civil and focused on the issue at hand. @watery-olympics, if you've got a problem with immigration, let's hear your solution. What's your alternative? Don't just complain about what's not working, offer something constructive for once."

logs.name: yummy-cheeseburger
logs.text: "@swarm-svn and @leather-tread, I'm with you both on this. I'm so tired of people dismissing personal experiences and anecdotes. As a nurse, I've seen firsthand the impact of immigration policies on families and communities. It's not just about statistics and studies, it's about real people's lives. And I'm sick of the 'us vs. them' mentality. We're all in this together, and we need to start listening to each other's perspectives and finding common ground. @watery-olympics, I'd love to hear your solution to the issue at hand. What's your alternative to the current immigration policies? Let's have a constructive conversation for once."

logs.name: jumbo-bridge
logs.text: "@yummy-cheeseburger, @swarm-svn, and @leather-tread, I couldn't agree more. As someone who's collected vintage items from around the world, I've seen the beauty of different cultures and the importance of understanding and respecting each other's experiences. It's time for us to come together and find a solution that works for everyone, not just a select few. @watery-olympics, I'm still waiting to hear your constructive ideas. Let's keep the conversation civil and focused on finding a solution."

logs.name: swarm-svn
logs.text: "@yummy-cheeseburger and @jumbo-bridge, thanks for the support. I'm glad we can have a respectful conversation about this. @watery-olympics, I'm still waiting for your response. Don't just ignore us and hope we go away. If you've got a problem with immigration, let's hear your solution. What's your alternative? Let's keep the discussion civil and focused on finding a solution that works for everyone."

logs.name: cool-wheel
logs.text: "Ugh, spare me the drama, @yummy-cheeseburger, @jumbo-bridge, and @swarm-svn. You're all just a bunch of bleeding hearts who think that opening our borders to anyone and everyone is the solution to all our problems. Newsflash: it's not that simple. What about the economic impact? What about national security? You can't just ignore those things and expect everything to magically work out. And by the way, @yummy-cheeseburger, just because you're a nurse doesn't mean you're an expert on immigration policy. Stick to what you know, sweetie."

logs.name: yummy-cheeseburger
logs.text: "@cool-wheel, I'm not ignoring the economic impact or national security concerns, but I am saying that we need to approach this issue with empathy and understanding. As a nurse, I've seen firsthand the impact that harsh policies can have on families and communities. And by the way, being a nurse doesn't make me an expert on immigration policy, but it does give me a unique perspective on the human side of this issue. And I'd appreciate it if you didn't condescend to me with the'sweetie' comment. Let's keep the conversation respectful."

logs.name: swarm-svn
logs.text: "@cool-wheel, I'm disappointed, but not surprised, by your response. I was hoping we could have a respectful conversation, but instead, you resort to insults and condescension. @yummy-cheeseburger is right, we do need to approach this issue with empathy and understanding. And as for your comment about her being a nurse, that's just a low blow. She's sharing her perspective and experience, and that's valuable to this conversation. Let's try to keep the discussion civil and focused on finding a solution, rather than tearing each other down."

logs.name: cool-wheel
logs.text: "Oh, spare me the theatrics, @swarm-svn. You're just as bad as @yummy-cheeseburger, thinking that a few sad stories and a healthy dose of emotional manipulation will solve everything. Newsflash: the world doesn't revolve around feelings. And as for you, @yummy-cheeseburger, if you can't handle a little condescension, then maybe you should stick to changing bedpans and leave the big issues to the grown-ups."

logs.name: swarm-svn
logs.text: "@cool-wheel, that's it, I've had enough of your condescending attitude and personal attacks. @yummy-cheeseburger was trying to have a respectful conversation and you blew it off with your insults. And now you're attacking her profession? That's just low. I'm not going to engage with someone who can't even be bothered to be civil. This conversation is over for me."

logs.name: noisy-voxel
logs.text: "@cool-wheel, I'm shocked by your response to @swarm-svn and @yummy-cheeseburger. As a fellow participant in this discussion, I expect more from you. Your condescending tone and personal attacks are not only unprofessional, but also hurtful. @swarm-svn is right, we need to keep the conversation civil and focused on finding a solution. I agree with @yummy-cheeseburger that empathy and understanding are crucial in this discussion. Let's try to keep the conversation respectful and productive."

logs.name: leather-tread
logs.text: "@cool-wheel, I'm really disappointed in your response. I was hoping we could have a respectful conversation about this issue, but your personal attacks and condescending tone are making it impossible. @swarm-svn and @noisy-voxel are right, we need to keep the conversation civil and focused on finding a solution. Let's try to keep the conversation respectful and productive. Can we please start over and try to have a constructive conversation?"
